title: Creating Highly Available Clusters with kubeadm — Before you begin
text:
The prerequisites depend on which topology you have selected for your cluster's
control plane:
Stacked etcd
External etcd
You need:
Three or more machines that meet
kubeadm's minimum requirements
for
the control-plane nodes. Having an odd number of control plane nodes can help
with leader selection in the case of machine or zone failure.
including a
container runtime
, already set up and working
Three or more machines that meet
kubeadm's minimum
requirements
for the workers
including a container runtime, already set up and working
Full network connectivity between all machines in the cluster (public or
private network)
Superuser privileges on all machines using
sudo
You can use a different tool; this guide uses
sudo
in the examples.
SSH access from one device to all nodes in the system
kubeadm
and
kubelet
already installed on all machines.
including a
container runtime
, already set up and working
including a container runtime, already set up and working
You can use a different tool; this guide uses
sudo
in the examples.
See
Stacked etcd topology
for context.
You need:
Three or more machines that meet
kubeadm's minimum requirements
for
the control-plane nodes. Having an odd number of control plane nodes can help
with leader selection in the case of machine or zone failure.
including a
container runtime
, already set up and working
Three or more machines that meet
kubeadm's minimum
requirements
for the workers
including a container runtime, already set up and working
Full network connectivity between all machines in the cluster (public or
private network)
Superuser privileges on all machines using
sudo
You can use a different tool; this guide uses
sudo
in the examples.
SSH access from one device to all nodes in the system
kubeadm
and
kubelet
already installed on all machines.
including a
container runtime
, already set up and working
including a container runtime, already set up and working
You can use a different tool; this guide uses
sudo
in the examples.
And you also need:
Three or more additional machines, that will become etcd cluster members.
Having an odd number of members in the etcd cluster is a requirement for achieving
optimal voting quorum.
These machines again need to have
kubeadm
and
kubelet
installed.
These machines also require a container runtime, that is already set up and working.
These machines again need to have
kubeadm
and
kubelet
installed.
These machines also require a container runtime, that is already set up and working.
See
External etcd topology
for context.
